title: Welcome to the Company
content:
Welcome to our company! This comprehensive guide will help you navigate your first days and weeks with us. We are excited to have you join our team.

## Your First Day

1. Arrive at 9 AM and go to the reception
2. Your badge will be ready
3. HR will greet you and complete paperwork
4. Your manager will show you around

## First Week

- Complete all required training
- Meet your team members
- Set up your workstation
- Review company policies

## Resources

- Employee handbook
- IT help desk
- HR contact: hr@company.com

We hope you enjoy your journey with us!
excerpt: Welcome guide for all new employees joining our company

title: Onboarding Checklist
content:
Complete this checklist during your first week. This is your roadmap to getting started successfully.

## Day 1
- [ ] Get badge and building access
- [ ] Set up email and Slack
- [ ] Meet your manager
- [ ] Complete HR paperwork

## Week 1
- [ ] Complete security training
- [ ] Set up development environment
- [ ] Meet your team
- [ ] Review team documentation

## First Month
- [ ] Complete onboarding checklist
- [ ] Attend first team meeting
- [ ] Submit first piece of work
- [ ] Complete 30-day check-in

Contact your manager or HR if you need help!
excerpt: Onboarding tasks checklist for your first week

title: Development Environment Setup
content:
Follow these steps to configure your local development environment.

## Prerequisites
- macOS, Linux, or Windows with WSL
- Admin access to install software

## Step 1: Install Tools
```bash
# Install Homebrew (macOS)
/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"

# Install required tools
brew install git docker python node
```

## Step 2: Clone Repositories
```bash
git clone git@github.com:company/main-repo.git
cd main-repo
npm install
```

## Step 3: Configure Environment
Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and fill in the values.

## Step 4: Run Development Server
```bash
npm run dev
```

Contact IT if you encounter any issues!
excerpt: Step-by-step guide to setting up your development environment

title: Code Review Best Practices
content:
Good code reviews improve quality and share knowledge across the team.

## For Reviewers

1. **Be constructive** - Focus on the code, not the person
2. **Ask questions** - Don't assume, clarify
3. **Suggest improvements** - Provide alternatives, not just criticism
4. **Approve when ready** - Don't block on style issues

## For Authors

1. **Keep PRs small** - Easier to review
2. **Write clear descriptions** - Explain what and why
3. **Respond to feedback** - Address all comments
4. **Don't take it personally** - Feedback is about code

## Checklist
- [ ] Code compiles and tests pass
- [ ] No security vulnerabilities
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] Tests added/updated
- [ ] Code follows style guide

title: Git Branching Strategy
content:
We use trunk-based development with short-lived feature branches.

## Main Branches
- `main` - Production-ready code
- `develop` - Integration branch

## Feature Branches
```bash
git checkout -b feature/TICKET-123-description
```

## Workflow
1. Create branch from `develop`
2. Make small, frequent commits
3. Push and create PR
4. Get code review
5. Merge to `develop`
6. Deploy to staging
7. Merge to `main` for production

## Naming Conventions
- `feature/TICKET-123-description`
- `bugfix/TICKET-456-description`
- `hotfix/production-issue`
excerpt: Our Git workflow and branching conventions

title: Company Leave Policy
content:
Our leave policy ensures work-life balance for all employees.

## Annual Leave
- 28 days paid vacation per year
- Accrues monthly (2.33 days/month)
- Maximum carryover: 5 days

## Sick Leave
- Unlimited sick leave with doctor note
- Notify manager within 2 hours of start time

## Parental Leave
- 16 weeks paid for primary caregiver
- 8 weeks paid for secondary caregiver

## How to Request Leave
1. Submit request in HR system
2. Get manager approval
3. Update team calendar

## Holiday Calendar
Check the company calendar for public holidays.
excerpt: Overview of employee leave and vacation policies

title: Remote Work Policy
content:
We support flexible work arrangements including remote work.

## Guidelines
- Up to 3 days remote work per week
- Coordinate with team lead for coverage
- Ensure reliable internet connection
- Be available during core hours (10 AM - 4 PM)

## Equipment
- Company provides laptop
- Additional equipment requires approval
- Ergonomic assessment available

## Communication
- Respond to messages within 2 hours during work hours
- Keep calendar updated with availability
- Attend all required meetings

## Security
- Use VPN for accessing company resources
- Don't work from public WiFi without VPN
- Keep your screen locked when away

title: Code of Conduct
content:
Our code of conduct outlines expectations for all employees.

## Core Values
- **Respect** - Treat everyone with dignity
- **Integrity** - Be honest and ethical
- **Collaboration** - Work together effectively
- **Excellence** - Strive for quality

## Expected Behaviors
- Communicate professionally
- Listen actively and openly
- Give and accept constructive feedback
- Support colleagues' growth

## Unacceptable Behaviors
- Harassment or discrimination
- Bullying or intimidation
- Sharing confidential information
- Misuse of company resources

## Reporting
Report concerns to HR or use the anonymous hotline.
excerpt: Employee code of conduct and professional behavior guidelines

title: Data Security Policy
content:
Protecting company data is everyone's responsibility.

## Password Requirements
- Minimum 12 characters
- Use a password manager
- Never share passwords

## Data Classification
- **Public** - Marketing materials
- **Internal** - Internal communications
- **Confidential** - Business data
- **Restricted** - PII, financial data

## Security Practices
1. Use two-factor authentication
2. Lock your computer when away
3. Encrypt sensitive files
4. Don't click suspicious links
5. Report security incidents immediately

## Incident Response
Contact security@company.com immediately if you suspect a breach.

title: Equal Opportunity Policy
content:
We are an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity.

## Our Commitment
We do not discriminate based on:
- Race, color, or ethnicity
- Gender or gender identity
- Age
- Religion
- Disability
- Sexual orientation
- National origin

## Recruitment
- Fair and unbiased hiring process
- Skills-based assessments
- Diverse interview panels

## Workplace
- Harassment-free environment
- Equal growth opportunities
- Pay equity across roles

## Reporting
Report discrimination to HR or use the anonymous reporting system.
excerpt: Our commitment to diversity and equal opportunity employment

title: API Design Guidelines
excerpt: Standards and best practices for API design

title: Testing Best Practices
content:
Quality testing ensures reliable software delivery.

## Test Pyramid
- **Unit Tests** - 70% - Fast, isolated
- **Integration Tests** - 20% - Test components
- **E2E Tests** - 10% - Full workflows

## Writing Tests
1. Test behavior, not implementation
2. Use descriptive test names
3. Follow AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert)
4. Keep tests independent
5. Aim for high coverage

## CI/CD Integration
- Run tests on every PR
- Block merges on failures
- Generate coverage reports

## Tools
- Unit: pytest, Jest
- Integration: Testcontainers
- E2E: Playwright, Cypress

title: Performance Review Process
content:
Our performance review process ensures fair and growth-oriented evaluations.

## Timeline
- **January** - Goal setting
- **June** - Mid-year review
- **December** - Annual review

## Review Components
1. Self-assessment
2. Manager evaluation
3. Peer feedback (360°)
4. Results calibration

## Rating Scale
- 5 - Exceptional
- 4 - Exceeds expectations
- 3 - Meets expectations
- 2 - Needs improvement
- 1 - Unsatisfactory

## Development Plans
Every employee receives a personalized development plan with training and growth opportunities.
excerpt: How performance reviews work at our company

title: Benefits Overview
content:
We offer comprehensive benefits to support your wellbeing.

## Health Insurance
- Medical, dental, vision
- Coverage starts day one
- Family coverage available

## Financial Benefits
- 401(k) with 4% match
- Stock options (vesting over 4 years)
- Annual bonus program

## Wellness
- Gym membership subsidy
- Mental health support
- Ergonomic equipment

## Professional Development
- Learning budget ($2000/year)
- Conference attendance
- Internal training programs

## Other Perks
- Free lunch (in-office days)
- Commuter benefits
- Generous parental leave

title: IT Security Tips
content:
Stay secure by following these IT security best practices.

## Password Security
- Use unique passwords for each account
- Enable two-factor authentication
- Use a password manager

## Email Safety
- Don't click suspicious links
- Verify sender addresses
- Report phishing attempts

## Device Security
- Keep software updated
- Use full-disk encryption
- Lock your device when away

## VPN Usage
- Always use VPN on public WiFi
- Connect to VPN before accessing company resources
- Report connection issues

## Reporting
Contact IT security immediately if you notice suspicious activity.
excerpt: Essential IT security practices for employees
